title: lora_l2.c + lichen_l2.c: EUI-64 pointer aliasing across module boundary
description:
Cross-file interaction bug extending project-LICHEN-d7ub.16: lichen_lora_l2_get_eui64() returns internal pointer without mutex, and lichen_l2.c uses it unsafely.

lora_l2.c:1141-1148 returns &lora_data.eui64 without holding lora_mutex.
lichen_l2.c:1487-1500 calls get_eui64() and copies to iface_link_addr.
lichen_l2.c:1070 calls get_eui64() during enable path.

RACE WINDOW in enable path (not present at iface_init time):
1. Thread A: lichen_l2_enable(true) at line 1070 calls get_eui64()
2. Thread A: Gets valid pointer
3. Thread B: calls lichen_lora_l2_deinit() transitioning to UNINIT
4. Thread B: lora_l2.c zeros lora_data.eui64 at line 941
5. Thread A: memcpy at line 1079 copies partially zeroed data

At iface_init time this is safe (runs before any other thread). But enable() can race with deinit() from another thread. The early needs_reinit() check at line 1034 doesn't protect against concurrent deinit that starts AFTER the check.

FIX: Copy EUI-64 under lora_mutex protection, either by changing get_eui64() API (as .16 suggests) or by adding post-copy validation in lichen_l2.c that the module didn't transition to UNINIT.
close_reason: Added mutex-held EUI-64 copy API and migrated local call sites away from internal-pointer aliasing
